once and FRAGOs consume them by reference.

polygon_vertices.description: Ordered polygon vertices (coordinate strings); used when the zone is non-circular.

Annex.description: An OPORD annex identified by a canonical A–Z letter per FM 6-0 / ATP 5-0.2-1.

letter.title: Letter

title.description: Annex title; if omitted, the validator fills in the canonical title from FM 6-0 (e.g. 'Task Organization' for Annex A).
title.title: Title

status.description: Computed automatically from content and letter. Derived as: 'not_used' for reserved letters (I, O, T, W, X, Y); 'published' when the annex has body/appendices/distribution list/callsigns; 'omitted' when the annex is listed but carries no content. Setting this field manually is allowed but usually unnecessary.

appendices.title: Appendices

anyOf.description: Typed structured-content body for an Annex. Discriminated on the 'letter' field (A–Z); reserved letters I/O/T/W/X/Y have no typed body.

typed_body.description: Optional structured-content layer with annex-specific doctrinal fields. Discriminated by annex letter; reserved letters (I, O, T, W, X, Y) must leave this None.
typed_body.title: Typed Body

JPITLEntry.description:
One row on the Joint Prioritized Integrated Target List (JPITL) per JP 3-60 / ATP 3-60.

The JPITL is the JFC-approved, prioritized, integrated target list across
all joint force components. Each row carries approval state, engagement
means, and commander's effect guidance. TST entries live on a separate
list (:class:`TSTEntry`) because their authority and timeline differ.

NineLineCAS.description:
9-Line CAS brief — structured tactical brief passed from JTAC/FAC(A)
to CAS aircraft per JP 3-09.3 / AFTTP 3-2.6.

Line order follows doctrinal format: IP → heading → distance → target
elevation → target description → target location → mark → friendlies →
egress. Remarks follow after line 9 (threats, hazards, laser code, TOT,
danger-close ground-commander initials, terminal-control type).

target_description.title: Target Description

target_location.description: Target location — MGRS, L/L, or offset from IP (bearing/distance). Match the format in use for the AO.
target_location.title: Target Location

mark_type.description: How the target will be marked for the aircraft.
mark_type.title: Mark Type

laser_code.description: 4-digit laser PRF code (e.g. '1688') when mark_type is LASER.

Weather.description:
Paragraph 1.b.(2) Weather — five military aspects, light, forecast, climate, effects.

Per FM 6-0 (May 2022) Appendix D and ATP 2-01.3 Ch 3, the weather
sub-paragraph must cover the five military aspects of weather
(visibility, winds, precipitation, cloud cover, temperature/humidity),
a light table, AND for multi-day operations a forecast keyed to
phases or days — a single snapshot is insufficient for campaign-
scale OPORDs. ``effects_on_operations`` captures the doctrinally
required 'so what' analysis. ``climate_baseline`` provides the
seasonal norm when the forecast confidence is low or the AO is
unfamiliar. The SWO / tactical weather team produces the underlying
Staff Weather Product; this paragraph is the executive summary
with the tables typically living in Annex B (Intelligence).

visibility.description: Visibility conditions and effects on operations (fog, haze, obscurants, illumination limits).
visibility.title: Visibility

winds.description: Surface and aloft winds with effects on aviation, fires, smoke, and CBRN employment.
winds.title: Winds

precipitation.title: Precipitation

cloud_cover.description: Ceiling and cloud coverage with effects on aviation, ISR, and close air support.
cloud_cover.title: Cloud Cover

temperature_and_humidity.description: Temperature and humidity ranges with effects on personnel, equipment, and heat/cold casualty risk.
temperature_and_humidity.title: Temperature And Humidity

light_data.description: Light table single-line summary: BMNT, sunrise, sunset, EENT, moon phase, illumination %. Use ``light_data_structured`` or ``forecast_by_period[].light_data`` for field-level data.
light_data.title: Light Data

light_data_structured.description: Structured light data for the critical day/event of the operation.

climate_baseline.description: Seasonal/regional climatology baseline for the AO (prevailing winds, precipitation norms, temperature bands). Used as the reference against which the operation-period forecast is compared; most useful for unfamiliar AOs or operations > 72 hrs.
climate_baseline.title: Climate Baseline

forecast_by_period.description: Forecast rows keyed to phases or D+N days for the full operation period. Doctrinally expected for multi-day / phased campaigns.
forecast_by_period.title: Forecast By Period

effects_on_operations.description: Consolidated 'so what' for the base order: how weather across the operation period affects friendly and enemy COAs and warfighting functions. Doctrinally required per ATP 2-01.3 Ch 3.
effects_on_operations.title: Effects On Operations

forecast_confidence.description: Forecast confidence level (LOW / MODERATE / HIGH) with rationale — drives branch planning around weather-dependent assets.
forecast_confidence.title: Forecast Confidence

swo_product_reference.description: Pointer to the authoritative SWO / tactical weather team product (e.g. 'Annex B, App 1, Tab B (Weather), SWO Product 26-04-WX-015').
swo_product_reference.title: Swo Product Reference

Weather.title: Weather

WeatherPeriodForecast.description:
One row of the operation-period weather forecast.

Per FM 6-0 / ATP 2-01.3, multi-day or phased operations require a
forecast covering the full operation period — typically keyed to
phase or D+N day — not a single snapshot. Each row carries the 5
aspects, optional structured light data, and an effects-on-operations
'so what' line. Produced by the Staff Weather Officer (SWO) /
G-2/S-2 weather team.

phase_or_day.description: Phase label or D+N day the forecast covers (e.g. 'Phase II / D+1', 'D-Day', 'H-6 to H+12').
phase_or_day.title: Phase Or Day

period_start.description: Period start DTG or date (e.g. '190500ZAPR26' or '2026-04-19').
period_start.title: Period Start

cloud_cover.description: Cloud cover / ceiling forecast with effects.
cloud_cover.title: Cloud Cover

temperature_and_humidity.description: Temperature (Hi/Lo) and humidity for this period.
temperature_and_humidity.title: Temperature And Humidity

light_data.description: Structured light data covering this period (if period ≤ 24 hrs) or the most operationally critical day in the window.

effects_on_operations.description: The 'so what' — net effect on friendly and enemy operations and specific warfighting functions (aviation, fires, ISR, optics, mobility, CBRN).
effects_on_operations.title: Effects On Operations

WeatherPeriodForecast.title: WeatherPeriodForecast

mission.description: Paragraph 2 — Mission statement: a single sentence answering who, what (task), when, where, and why (purpose).

annexes.description: Ordered list of annexes attached to this order; gaps are filled canonically by annex_directory().
annexes.title: Annexes
